title: Modular Stackable Dollhouse
summary: A build-your-own-set dollhouse system — plywood room boxes, railed decks, and peaked roof modules that stack in any combination.
description:
The whole idea here is a kit of parts rather than one fixed design: rooms, decks, and roof modules are all built to the same footprint, so any combination stacks cleanly and the "finished" dollhouse is really just however many modules you decide to build. That makes this a genuinely good project to build incrementally — start with two or three rooms and a roof, and add more later without redesigning anything.

The roof module is the one piece that needs real care: its moulding frame has mitered 45° corners, and it has to be checked square against an actual room module before the glue sets, not just squared on its own. A roof that's square in isolation but doesn't match the room footprint will rock or gap when stacked. Paint the 1x pieces before assembly wherever the plan calls for it — reaching a brush into an assembled dollhouse room is far harder than painting flat stock.
timeLabel: 4–6 hrs per basic set (room + deck + roof)

tools.note: Handy for ripping the plywood strips to width if you're not buying pre-ripped stock.

materials.name: Plywood, 1/2", ripped into 7-3/4" strips
materials.note: Room walls, deck, and roof floor — quantity depends on how many modules you build.

materials.name: Paint
materials.note: Apply to 1x pieces before assembly wherever possible.

cutList.note: One end cut at 45°, long-point measurement.
One end cut at 45°, long-point measurement.
Ends cut to a point at 45°.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two room walls at 15-1/2" and one deck floor at 15-1/2" from the Plywood. Cut four room frame pieces at 7-3/4" from the Pine, 1x4. Cut two deck railing, long sides at 14", four deck railing, short sides at 7-3/4", four deck railing, corner posts at 2", four roof frame, long sides at 14", two roof frame, gable long at 6-3/4" and two roof frame, gable short at 6" from the Moulding. Cut twelve deck railing spindles at 2-1/2" from the Wood dowel. Cut two roof peak trim at 11" from the Pine, 1x2. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Build a room module
steps.body: A room module is a plywood box on a 1x frame: the two 15-1/2" x 7-3/4" plywood panels are its floor and ceiling, and two of the 7-3/4" 1x4 frame pieces stand between them at the ends. The 7-3/4" pieces run the full depth and the panels close over them, which fixes the module footprint at 15-1/2" x 7-3/4". Paint the 1x pieces before assembly wherever you can; it is far easier to get clean coverage on flat stock than inside an assembled room. Glue and 1-1/4" brad-nail or screw through the plywood into the frame pieces, checking the box for square across its diagonals before the glue grabs.

steps.title: Build a deck module with railing
steps.body: The deck is the 15-1/2" x 7-3/4" plywood floor with a moulding railing built around it. Lay the railing out with two 7-3/4" short sides running the full depth at the ends and the two 14" long sides fitting between them, which gives a rail rectangle 15-1/2" long overall and 7-3/4" deep. Drill the 1/4" dowel holes 1/4" deep in the rails; with a 1/4" socket at each end, the 2-1/2" spindles leave 2" of clear rise between the rails, matching the 2" corner posts. Glue the spindles into their holes, glue and nail the rail frame together with the corner posts at the four corners, then fix the whole railing down to the plywood floor with 1-1/4" brad nails.

steps.title: Build a roof module
steps.body: Build the roof frame's base rectangle to the same footprint as the module below it: two 14" long sides fitted between a pair of 7-3/4" end members, giving a 15-1/2" outside length and a 7-3/4" depth. Cut the gable pieces' 45-degree ends on the miter saw, with the 6-3/4" gable longs and 6" gable shorts measured to the long point, and bring them together at the peak. Cap the ridge with the two 11" 1x2 peak trims, whose ends are cut to a point at 45 degrees. Glue and 1-1/4" brad-nail throughout, and before the glue sets, set the roof assembly down on an actual room module and confirm it is square against the room's 15-1/2" x 7-3/4" footprint. The source does not say where the remaining two 14" long sides go, so dry-fit them against your assembly and decide rather than nailing on a guess.

steps.title: Stack and add furnishings
steps.body: Stack your room, deck and roof modules in any combination; every core module should land on the same 15-1/2" x 7-3/4" footprint. Set each new module down dry and check it sits flat on the one below before you commit to a finish; a module that gaps or rocks is telling you its frame is out of square. Sand every edge and corner that a child will handle, and paint or finish each module separately so you can add to the set later without masking off what you already built. Add furnishings and details as separate small builds once the core modules are done.

images.alt: Modular stackable wooden dollhouse with interchangeable room, deck, and roof pieces
